history: The client is a PhD student in a science field with a strong background in music, having led a college band and currently participating in a jazz group. He declined a leadership position in the jazz group due to mental health struggles and a sense of disconnection from colleagues in his scientific work environment. He has a deep engagement with mathematics, describing it as an intellectual activity, and finds beauty in the logical progression of proofs. He feels slightly self-conscious about his preoccupation with mathematics and often feels disconnected from the immediate world.
intermediate_belief: If I can't progress smoothly during rehearsals, then I am not competent enough to be part of the group.
intermediate_belief_depression: If I struggle during rehearsals, it means I will never be able to fit in or succeed.
coping_strategies: The client tends to immerse himself in mathematics, creating a 'bubble' that disconnects him from the immediate world. He also avoids leadership roles and writing, which previously helped him explore personal ideas.
situation: Experiencing difficulty during jazz rehearsals, requiring repeated attempts to progress.
auto_thought: I'm struggling again; I must not be good enough for this group.
behavior: He hesitates to take initiative during rehearsals and avoids suggesting ideas. He may withdraw from the group, focusing instead on his mathematical pursuits.
